name: Pam's Malfuf (Middle Eastern Cabbage Rolls)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup regular or basmati rice, uncooked
recipeInstructions:
1. Core cabbage sparingly and boil in a large pot of water over medium heat until leaves begin to come apart. Remove leaves as they soften and separate.
2. When cool enough to handle, thin out the center vein of each leaf with a paring knife.
3. Drain tomatoes, reserving liquid, and dice. Mix the ground beef with onions, garlic, parsley, tomatoes, seasoned salt, pepper, egg, rice, and 1/4 cup oil.
4. Spoon the mixture onto each cabbage leaf at the thicker edge. Roll either tucking sides in once or like a cigar—roll snugly but with enough room for the rice to expand.
5. Coarsely chop up the leftover cabbage and place it in the bottom of a large pot or Dutch oven (This will prevent the cabbage rolls from scorching).
6. Place cabbage rolls on top of chopped cabbage.
7. Combine the reserved juice from the canned tomatoes with the large can of tomato juice, 4 tablespoons oil, salt, and pepper. Pour over the cabbage rolls. The liquid should cover or nearly cover the rolls.
8. Cook, covered, over medium heat for 1 hour. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for a further 30 minutes.
